process_selection.description: Decision matrix for choosing the right welding process based on user needs. Extracted from the selection chart and manual specifications.

Flux-Cored.weld_cleanliness: More spatter (slag must be chipped off)
advantages: Ideal for outdoor or windy conditions
Forgiving on rusty or dirty steels
Flux-Cored.best_for: Beginners, outdoor work, dirty/rusty metal, general fabrication

MIG.best_for: Clean indoor work, thin materials, automotive, sheet metal, production speed

Stick.weld_cleanliness: More spatter (slag must be chipped off)
advantages: Ideal for outdoor or windy conditions
Forgiving on rusty or dirty steels
Stick.best_for: Thicker materials, outdoor work, structural steel, repair work

ac_tig: Magnesium Alloys (requires AC TIG — not available on this machine's DC-only TIG)

advantages: Can be used on a variety of materials
TIG.best_for: Precision work, thin materials, aesthetics, stainless steel, chrome moly
TIG.limitations_on_this_machine: DC TIG only (lift-start). No AC TIG for aluminum. No high-frequency start. Training beyond manual scope required.

mild_steel.best_choice_beginner: MIG (easiest to learn) or Flux-Cored (no gas setup)

stainless_steel.note: Stainless wire is less flexible — keep gun cable straight during feeding

aluminum.requirements: Optional Spool Gun (sold separately) + 100% Argon gas
aluminum.note: AC TIG would be ideal for aluminum but this machine only has DC TIG. MIG with spool gun is the available option.

castings.note: Stick welding with appropriate electrode type

very_thin.reason: Better heat control, less burn-through risk
very_thin.tips: Use lower amperage, faster travel speed, longer CTWD

thin.reason: All wire processes work, TIG gives cleanest result

medium.reason: Sweet spot for all four processes

thick.reason: Need higher amperage and deeper penetration
thick.tips: Bevel thick workpieces, allow slight gap, weld in several passes

quick_decision_tree.question: Are you working outdoors or in windy conditions?
quick_decision_tree.if_yes: Use Flux-Cored (no gas, wind-tolerant) or Stick

quick_decision_tree.question: What material are you welding?
quick_decision_tree.if_aluminum: MIG with optional Spool Gun + 100% Argon

quick_decision_tree.question: How clean do you need the weld?
What's your skill level?
quick_decision_tree.if_beginner: MIG (easiest to learn) or Flux-Cored (simplest setup, no gas)
quick_decision_tree.if_advanced: TIG for precision, any process for general work
